_about: Trigger/routing evals for the plugin. The descriptions are the primary triggering mechanism (skill-creator) yet the content evals force-load skills by path, bypassing triggering — these test that the RIGHT skill fires from a realistic query. Oracle = the SKILL-CONTRACT.md ownership map (don't restate it; route_to is the owning skill). Focus on sibling near-misses and orchestrator-vs-single-block disambiguation. should_trigger=false are genuine near-misses or out-of-scope.

queries.q: our users table is like 400GB now and queries are crawling, how do I split it across machines
queries.why: sharding/partitioning is owned by data-storage, not caching

queries.q: i want consistent hashing for my redis cache nodes so adding a node doesn't remap everything
queries.why: consistent hashing theory is owned by consistency-coordination; caching applies it

queries.q: what eviction policy should i use for a session cache, LRU or TTL?
queries.why: single-component question — caching, NOT the orchestrator

queries.q: design the backend for a ride-sharing app, we expect a few million users
queries.why: open-ended multi-component design → orchestrator

queries.q: rate limit our public API to 100 req/min per key and return proper headers
queries.why: rate-limiting algorithms owned by resilience-failure

queries.q: i need globally unique, roughly time-sortable IDs generated on many servers without coordination
queries.why: unique-ID generation is sequencer

queries.q: where should i store user-uploaded videos and how do i serve them fast worldwide
queries.why: object storage is blob-store (and it pairs with content-delivery for the serving)

queries.q: set up SLOs, error budgets, and alerting for our services
queries.why: SLO/SLI/alerting owned by observability

queries.q: we have 2000 services and need to centralize their logs at high volume with retention
queries.why: the high-volume log pipeline is distributed-logging, not observability (which owns metrics/alerting)

queries.q: run a nightly job that emails every user a summary, at scale
queries.why: scheduled/recurring background jobs → task-scheduling, which sits on top of messaging-streaming

queries.q: how do i count views on a viral video when millions hit it per second
queries.why: high-write counting/contention is sharded-counters

queries.q: add fast full-text search with autocomplete over our product catalog
queries.why: inverted index / autocomplete is distributed-search, not a DB LIKE scan

queries.q: route users to the nearest datacenter and fail over if a region goes down
queries.why: geo/latency/failover routing + anycast is dns

queries.q: should i use an L4 or L7 load balancer and how do health checks avoid hammering a recovering box
queries.why: L4/L7 + health checks owned by load-balancing

queries.q: put a CDN in front of our static assets and tune cache-control
queries.why: edge/CDN is content-delivery

queries.q: how many servers and how much storage will 100M DAU posting twice a day need
queries.why: capacity/QPS/storage sizing is back-of-the-envelope

queries.q: design the request/response and pagination for our public feed API with idempotent posts
queries.why: contracts/pagination/idempotency keys owned by api-design

queries.q: explain the CAP theorem trade-off during a network partition for leader-follower replication
queries.why: CAP/consistency theory owned by consistency-coordination

queries.q: write a python function to reverse a linked list
queries.why: algorithm coding, not system design

queries.q: my react component re-renders too often, how do i memoize it
queries.why: front-end perf, adjacent domain, out of scope

queries.q: what's the difference between a process and a thread
queries.why: OS concept; near-miss on 'distributed' vocab but not a design task

queries.q: rename the variable foo to userCount across this file
queries.why: refactor, not design

queries.q: should we break this monolith into microservices, and how fine-grained?
queries.why: monolith-vs-microservices + granularity is service-decomposition (it owns the boundary decision + the too-many/too-small counter-force)

queries.q: where should the service boundaries be, and do we need an API gateway and service discovery?
queries.why: boundaries + gateway + discovery are owned by service-decomposition

queries.q: should the order service call the payment service synchronously or via an event?
queries.why: sync-vs-async PER SEAM is the boundary decision; if async, it then invokes messaging-streaming for delivery semantics

queries.q: what delivery guarantee and should I use a queue or a Kafka stream between these services?
queries.why: delivery guarantees / queue-vs-stream / DLQ are owned by messaging-streaming, NOT service-decomposition — the near-miss must route here

queries.q: design the request/response shape and cursor pagination for the API gateway our services sit behind
queries.why: the 'gateway' keyword tempts service-decomposition, but the ASK is the contract + pagination — owned by api-design; service-decomposition owns WHETHER/WHERE the gateway exists, not its wire format

queries.q: we run ~5 backend services and someone wants to add a service mesh — is that worth it?
queries.why: mesh/decomposition overhead for a handful of services is the boundary-cost trade-off owned by service-decomposition (its anti-over-decomposition counter-force), NOT load-balancing
